phase1.explanation:
🎮 **Unity handles both 2D and 3D games — but they work differently.**

**2D Games:**
- Objects exist on a flat plane (X and Y axes)
- Uses sprites (2D images) instead of 3D models
- Physics is 2D (Rigidbody2D, Collider2D)
- Common genres: platformers, top-down games, puzzle games

**3D Games:**
- Objects exist in 3D space (X, Y, and Z axes)
- Uses 3D models (meshes)
- Physics is 3D (Rigidbody, Collider)
- Common genres: first-person, third-person, open-world

**Sharp-chan says:**
"2D is simpler and faster to develop. It's perfect for learning game dev. Every concept you learn in 2D transfers to 3D."

**Unity 2D vs 3D Project Settings:**

| Setting | 2D | 3D |
|---------|----|----|
| **Project Template** | 2D Core | 3D Core |
| **Main Camera** | Orthographic | Perspective |
| **Sprite Renderer** | Yes | No (uses Mesh Renderer) |
| **Physics** | Rigidbody2D | Rigidbody |
| **Colliders** | Collider2D | Collider |
| **Default GameObject** | Sprite | Cube |

**When to use 2D:**
- Platformers (Celeste, Hollow Knight)
- Top-down games (Stardew Valley)
- Puzzle games (Tetris, Bejeweled)
- Mobile games (Flappy Bird, Candy Crush)

**When to use 3D:**
- First-person games (FPS)
- Third-person games (Action RPGs)
- Open-world games
- VR/AR experiences

**Sharp-chan says:**
"For this course, we're focusing on 2D. It's the fastest way to learn game development and ship a complete game."

phase2.explanation:
Conceptual lesson — no code to break!

**Common misconception:** "3D games are always better than 2D games."

Actually: 2D games have their own charm, simplicity, and audience. Some of the most successful games of all time are 2D.

**Common misconception:** "2D games are easier than 3D games."

Actually: 2D games can be just as complex — they're just different. The design challenges are different, not easier.

**Sharp-chan says:**
"Don't underestimate 2D games. Many of the best games ever made are 2D."

phase3.prompt:
Which of these is TRUE about 2D games in Unity?

A) 2D games use Rigidbody (not Rigidbody2D)
B) 2D games use SpriteRenderer to display images
C) 2D games use 3D models
D) 2D games use perspective cameras

Select the CORRECT answer.
dialogueHints: Think about how 2D objects are displayed.
They use a specific renderer component.
